title: Vriesea heliconioides
intro: Vriesea heliconioides, also known as the "Heliconia Bromeliad" or "Sun Bromeliad", is a species of flowering plant in the bromeliad family, characterized by its bright yellow and red bracts and small white flowers. Traditionally, Vriesea heliconioides has been used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, inflammation, and respiratory issues, due to its potential health benefits. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Vriesea heliconioides is often prepared as a tea or infusion, or used in the form of a tincture or syrup to harness its health benefits.

This family comprises over 3,000 species of plants, many of which are epiphytes, similar to Vriesea heliconioides. The genus Vriesea is a group of bromeliads native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. Vriesea heliconioides is a species within this genus and is native to the tropical forests of Central America.
